name: Ration Card (NFSA)
target_description: Approximately 81 crore persons. AAY for poorest of poor (35 kg/household/month). PHH for remaining covered population (5 kg/person/month). State governments identify beneficiaries.
ministry: Department of Food and Public Distribution, Ministry of Consumer Affairs

rules.description: Household income should be below state-specified threshold (e.g., Delhi: <₹1 lakh/year)
rules.ambiguity_note: Real eligibility is entirely state-specific. Delhi example: income <₹1 lakh/year. Central rules minimal.

rules.description: Applicant belongs to a vulnerable category (SC/ST, widow, disabled, or not a government employee)
rules.ambiguity_note: Vulnerable groups: SC/ST, widows, disabled, slum residents, shelterless, single women, construction workers, rickshaw drivers, domestic workers, ragpickers.

rules.description: Applicant should not already have an active ration card
rules.ambiguity_note: Should not have existing ration card from another state. One card per family.

rules.description: Applicant must have Aadhaar for ONORC portability
rules.ambiguity_note: Aadhaar required for all family members for ONORC portability.

rules.description: Income tax payers are excluded — NFSA targets economically weaker sections
rules.ambiguity_note: NFSA coverage is for the bottom 67% — income tax payers are implicitly excluded as they are above the economic threshold.

required_documents.document: Aadhaar Cards of all family members
Residence Proof if different from Aadhaar address

benefit_summary: AAY: 35 kg food grain per household per month at ₹3/2 per kg (rice/wheat) + 1 kg subsidised sugar. PHH: 5 kg per person per month at ₹3/2/1 per kg (rice/wheat/coarse grain).
